source_item.title: Reliance Jio Q1 Results: IPO-bound telco's net profit rises 9% YoY to Rs 7,764 crore; ARPU climbs to Rs 215.6

slide1_hook.label: Jio's Profit Rises 9% Before Its IPO
slide1_hook.subhead: Broadband and 5G growth boost telecom arm ahead of listing.

slide2_backstory.label: THE BACKSTORY
slide2_backstory.headline: From Rs250/GB To India's Broadband King
slide2_backstory.body: Jio launched in 2016 with free data and calls, crashing data prices from Rs 250 per GB to under Rs 10. In 2020, Reliance spun it off as Jio Platforms and raised over $20 billion from Meta, Google, and others.

slide3_news.label: THE UPDATE
slide3_news.headline: Profit Up 9%, ARPU Climbs To Rs215.6
slide3_news.body: Jio's Q1 net profit rose 9.2% to Rs 7,764 crore, on revenue up 12% to Rs 45,961 crore. ARPU climbed to Rs 215.6, and Jio added 8.9 million users to reach 533.3 million.

slide4_why.label: WHY IT MATTERS
slide4_why.headline: Numbers That Matter For The IPO Pitch
bullets: Rising ARPU is the growth story for IPO investors
Broadband market share crossed 43%, now India's leader
AirFiber drove over 75% of new broadband signups
Akash Ambani touts Jio's top 20 patent portfolio

slide5_engage.label: YOUR TAKE
slide5_engage.headline: Jio wants to be valued as a tech company, not just a telecom operator, ahead of its IPO. Do these numbers back that up, or is it still just a phone company?
slide5_engage.cta: Follow for daily breakdowns of India's biggest business stories.
